acceptance_criteria: Iter61 is a clean blocked result with redacted CONSUMER_INVALID evidence.
The binding path injects bearer token and quota project at runtime without committed secrets.
At most two provider probes and at most $0.05 bounded spend occur.
No BattleSnake row, excluded pair, GPU, Sentinel mutation, cloud runner, or overclaim occurs.

evidence.notes: Records source-path support, runtime token binding, minimal probe outcome, calls, cost, redaction, and no-row controls.
Secret-safe runtime binding template for a future exact two-row retry.
Review records the bearer-token-only claim boundary.

falsifiers: The result must block if iter61 does not show the named access-path blocker.
The result must block if a secret-safe LiteLLM bearer-token/header path cannot be identified.
The result must fail if any BattleSnake row, excluded pair, GPU, Sentinel mutation, cloud runner, or overclaim occurs.
stated_goal: Recover the LiteLLM Vertex bearer-token/header path after iter61 showed that quota-project extra_headers alone still blocked.
